commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ce260c937721763a9372267b8d17c6635b2a4c45)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a non-Windows command-authorization candidate planner for POSIX shell wrappers and argv allowlist evaluation, updates allow-always behavior/tests, and teaches the web approval modal to render only allowed decisions.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source inspection: plugin approvals can declare restricted allowedDecisions, the Gateway rejects decisions outside that set, and the changed modal can hide Deny while cancel still submits deny. I did not run the UI locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This protected security-boundary PR needs maintainer review plus a small UI correctness fix before merge, not an automated cleanup close.

target.title: Revamp command authorization candidates
target.body:
## Summary

- add a candidate planner boundary for command authorization, including groups, trust modes, shell-wrapper transport metadata, and planned execution segments
- route the normal POSIX shell and non-Windows argv allowlist paths through planner candidates while leaving Windows and legacy fallback behavior in place
- preserve current approval storage, `curl | sh` stance, and UI display of the actual command while teaching the UI to honor `allowedDecisions`

## Behavior changes

- Static POSIX shell wrappers such as `sh -c 'git status'` can now be satisfied by the inner `git` candidate when the payload is static and safely parsed. Revert by making shell-wrapper groups fall back to the old wrapper-exact allowlist path.
- Shell-wrapper compound metadata can now expose inner candidates/segments instead of one outer wrapper segment. Safe-bin inline-chain rewriting still collapses back to wrapper metadata where execution requires it.
- Path-scoped shell-wrapper payloads, including later payload commands such as `sh -c 'git status && ./scripts/run.sh'`, no longer create reusable inner script trust. Revert by relaxing `canUseReusableWrapperPayloadCandidates`, with matching tests.
- Approval UI now hides decisions not allowed by the authorization request, so `ask=always` does not render an unusable Always Allow action.
- `curl URL | sh` / `curl URL | bash` behavior is preserved: manual allowlist needs both sides, allow-always persists `curl` only, and persisted `curl` alone does not satisfy a rerun.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Candidate-based command authorization for POSIX shell and non-Windows argv execution, including static shell-wrapper unwrapping, dynamic/path-scoped fallback, pipe-to-shell behavior, allow-always persistence, node-host execution, and UI approval decisions.

Real environment tested: Local macOS focused Vitest/tsgo/oxlint; isolated local OpenClaw UI proof; Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ks0aqt6a2pkx7fyvstftjxzh` with GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26104328069.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts --reporter=verbose
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run-plan.test.ts --reporter=verbose
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-plan.test.ts src/infra/exec-authorization-allowlist.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts --reporter=verbose
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts --reporter=verbose
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src ui packages
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: Focused local tests passed with 27 planner/allowlist tests, 50 node-host tests, 262 broader infra tests, 48 agent tests, and 74 UI tests. Core tsgo passed. Core oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors. Testbox `pnpm check:changed` exited 0.

Observed result after fix: Static shell wrappers can auto-run from trusted inner candidates, dynamic and path-scoped wrapper payloads remain approval-gated, mixed allowlist/safe-bin wrapper payloads are rebuilt before execution, and the approval UI renders only allowed decisions.

What was not tested: Full release validation was not run. Windows behavior stays on the legacy path and is covered only by existing targeted tests. The local UI proof used an isolated no-channel OpenClaw instance rather than real Slack/Discord listeners.

## UI proof notes

The local UI proof used an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and filtered a stale `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` line from the installed `openclaw-local-test` helper. The proof ran `printf openclaw-approval-proof`, showed the approval modal, clicked Always Allow, observed output `openclaw-approval-proof`, inspected the stored `/usr/bin/printf` allow-always approval with `lastUsedCommand` and `lastResolvedPath`, reran the same prompt, and confirmed no second approval modal appeared.
target.author: jesse-merhi

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
